turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm shows absolute convergence: gbest has not improved for 200 iterations (best_delta = 0), normalized diversity is ~1.22e-15, swarm radius ~1.5e-13, all velocities are effectively zero (velocity_zero_ratio = 1.0, velocity_norm_mean ~2.5e-14). Fitness values are nearly identical (std ~1.25e-14). No particles are at boundaries. The current gbest is 19.899, far above the known global optimum of Rastrigin (0.0). This indicates the swarm has collapsed into a local minimum. | [DIAGNOSE] The root cause is premature convergence to a deep local minimum typical of Rastrigin's highly multimodal landscape. The entire swarm has collapsed to a single point, removing all exploration capability. The stagnation for 200 iterations without any improvement confirms a complete loss of diversity. The extreme similarity of all particles rules out any ongoing local refinement — the swarm is dead. | [STRATEGIZE] To escape this trap, we must forcefully inject diversity. A random reinitialization of a substantial fraction of the swarm will populate new regions of the search space, potentially discovering better basins. Levy flights or mutations around the collapsed best would not be sufficient because the basin is a deep local minimum; they would likely recollapse or remain stuck. Resetting half the worst particles (all are equally bad here) to random positions and velocities will restart exploration while preserving the memory of the current best position. I will also set a moderate inertia weight (0.9) to maintain some momentum for exploration.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The immediate result shows no change in the global best (delta = 0.0). This is expected because the intervention preserved the best position while only reinitializing the worst particles. The key effect – restoring swarm diversity – is not reflected in the single‑step feedback, but injecting 15 random particles should drastically increase position variance and break the stagnation. The swarm now has the capacity to explore new regions of the landscape. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The diagnosis of premature convergence remains correct. The additional finding is that the entire swarm was collapsed to a single local attractor of depth ~19.9. A 50% reset is a strong perturbation that should diversify exploration. However, because the global best and the remaining converged particles still represent that local optimum, there is a risk that the reinitialized particles will be quickly pulled back if the cognitive and social forces are too strong. Monitoring whether the swarm escapes or reconverges is required before further action.
